# mtt prime: curated, opt-in, pointer-only KB digest
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init
stdout 'initialized'

# empty repo -> the actionable "no important notes" line (exit 0)
exec mtt prime
stdout 'no important notes'

# a mix of priorities; only high is primed by default
exec mtt note add a --priority high --title 'Auth' --body 'SECRET-BODY'
exec mtt note add b --priority medium
exec mtt note add c

exec mtt prime
stdout '# Knowledge base — important notes'
stdout '- \*\*a\*\*  \[high\]  — Auth'
stdout '1 of 1 important notes shown'
# bodies are NEVER emitted (pointer-only)
! stdout 'SECRET-BODY'
# medium/unset are not in the default digest
! stdout '\*\*b\*\*'
! stdout '\*\*c\*\*'

# --min-priority medium widens to a+b (still not the unset c); footer 2 of 2
exec mtt prime --min-priority medium
stdout '\*\*a\*\*'
stdout '\*\*b\*\*'
! stdout '\*\*c\*\*'
stdout '2 of 2 important notes shown'

# --limit caps the shown count but the footer reports the eligible total
exec mtt prime --min-priority medium --limit 1
stdout '1 of 2 important notes shown'

# an invalid --min-priority is a usage error
! exec mtt prime --min-priority bogus
stderr 'invalid --min-priority'

# --json emits a non-null array with priority + backlinks
exec mtt prime --json
stdout '"priority": "high"'
stdout '"backlinks": 0'
